channel.title: Green Curve Insights
channel.description: Daily ESG and climate compliance intelligence for Indian businesses.

item.title: CPCB Extends Interim Relief for Importers of 85 EEE Items: EPR Registration Deadline Now 30 June 2023
item.description: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has issued a clarificatory letter dated 9 May 2023 to the Customs authorities, extending interim arrangements for releasing imported consignments of producers of 85 categories of Electrical and Electronic Equipment (EEE) under the E-Waste (Management) Rules

item.title: CPCB Greenlights Customs Release for 85 EEE Item Importers: Undertaking-Based Clearance Under E-Waste Rules 2022
item.description: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has written to the Central Board of Excise and Customs allowing the release of imported consignments of 85 categories of Electrical and Electronic Equipment (EEE) covered under the new E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022, subject to an undertaking from the Pro

item.title: Plastic Waste Management (2nd Amendment) Rules 2022: EPR Tightens for Producers, Importers & Brand Owners
item.description: On 6 July 2022, the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) notified the Plastic Waste Management (Second Amendment) Rules, 2022, significantly strengthening the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) framework for plastic packaging. The amendment introduces clear definitions for

item.title: Guidelines on Extended Producer Responsibility for Plastic Packaging
item.description: The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has notified the Plastic Waste Management (Amendment) Rules, 2022, introducing legally binding Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) guidelines for plastic packaging. Producers, Importers, and Brand Owners (PIBOs) are now accountable for the en

item.title: ESG Index Inclusion as a Capital Strategy: What Indian CFOs and CSOs Must Understand About MSCI, FTSE4Good, and the $500M Demand Signal
item.description: Inclusion in major ESG indexes — MSCI ESG Leaders, FTSE4Good, S&P ESG — is no longer merely a reputational marker; it is a direct mechanism for generating incremental institutional demand for a company's equity, with documented examples of single index additions triggering $500 million in net inflow

item.title: Australia Raises Sustainability Reporting Thresholds: What India's BRSR Architecture and Listed Companies Should Read Into This Global Rollback Signal
item.description: Australia's 2026 Budget has proposed doubling the revenue and asset thresholds for mandatory sustainability reporting, effectively removing a significant cohort of smaller companies from its climate disclosure regime — a move framed explicitly as a A$10.2 billion per year regulatory burden reduction

item.title: Crew Carbon's $25M Raise Signals a Convergence of Wastewater Infrastructure and Carbon Markets — What Indian Utilities, BRSR Reporters, and Carbon Credit Buyers Must Understand Now
item.description: Crew Carbon, a Yale University spinout, has closed a $25 million Series A to scale its wastewater-integrated enhanced mineral weathering technology — a carbon removal pathway that generates verified, permanent CO2 removal credits sold to corporate buyers including JPMorganChase, Google, and Stripe,

item.title: HSBC's $4 Billion China Cleantech Facility: What India's CFOs and CSOs Must Understand About the Capital Race They Are Now In
item.description: HSBC's $4 billion Sustainability and Transition Credit Facility — announced on 18 May 2026 and reported by ESG Today (https://www.esgtoday.com/hsbc-launches-4-billion-facility-to-help-china-clean-tech-companies-scale-internationally/) — is designed to accelerate the international scaling of Mainland

item.title: BBVA's 99% Renewable Electricity and 83% Scope 1&2 Reduction: The Operational Decarbonisation Benchmark Indian Banks Cannot Ignore
item.description: Spanish banking group BBVA has disclosed 99% renewable electricity coverage across its global operations in 2025 and an 83% reduction in Scope 1 and 2 emissions since 2019 — a performance benchmark that directly pressures Indian listed banks and financial institutions now subject to mandatory BRSR C

item.title: Draft Plastic Waste Management Rules, 2022: Tightening EPR and Expanding Producer Accountability
item.description: The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) has published the Draft Plastic Waste Management (Third Amendment) Rules, 2021 — building on the EPR framework already issued in October 2021. The amendments sharpen definitions around plastic packaging, biodegradable plastics, recycler

item.title: Lightrock's $500M Accelerate7 Fund: What India's Clean Energy Companies, CFOs, and ESG Investors Must Understand About the New Emerging Market Capital Playbook
item.description: Lightrock's $500 million Accelerate7 fund — anchored by major energy corporates including Shell, Equinor, and TotalEnergies, and already invested in Indian companies SolarSquare and Euler Motors — signals a structural recalibration of private capital toward SDG 7-aligned growth-stage businesses in S

item.title: FTSE Russell–Planetrics Climate-Scenario Indices: Why Indian Institutional Investors and BSE 500 Boards Cannot Afford to Ignore Index-Level Climate Pricing
item.description: FTSE Russell and climate risk analytics platform Planetrics have signed an MoU to jointly develop climate-scenario-based indices across asset classes, with a launch anticipated before end-2026 — marking the first time scenario-modelled physical and transition climate risk will be embedded directly i

item.title: Sunraycer's $901M Texas Solar-Storage Finance Close: What the ERCOT Capital Stack Tells Indian Renewable Developers, CFOs, and ESG Investors
item.description: Sunraycer Renewables has closed a $901 million construction-to-term project finance facility — comprising a construction loan, tax credit bridge, and LC facility — to build 479.5 MWac of solar and 236.5 MWac of paired battery storage across three Texas projects, as reported by ESG Today (https://www

item.title: Crux's $500M Nuveen Facility: What the U.S. Tax Equity Market's 23% Growth Signals for India's Green Finance Architecture
item.description: Fintech platform Crux has closed a $500 million debt facility with Nuveen's infrastructure credit arm to scale U.S. clean energy tax equity and transferable tax credit investments — a market that hit $37 billion in 2025, growing 23% year-on-year. For Indian CFOs and CSOs, this is not a U.S.-only sto

item.title: Draft Notification on Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) for Plastic Packaging: What Businesses Need to Know
item.description: On 6 October 2021, the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) published a draft notification operationalizing Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) for plastic packaging under the Plastic Waste Management Rules, 2016. The framework places mandatory collection, recycling, and en

item.title: Plastic Waste Management (Amendment) Rules, 2021: India's Roadmap to Phasing Out Single-Use Plastics
item.description: The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change notified the Plastic Waste Management (Amendment) Rules, 2021 on 12 August 2021, setting a phased nationwide ban on identified single-use plastic (SUP) items from 1 July 2022 and raising the minimum thickness of plastic carry bags. The amendment

item.title: E-Waste Third Amendment Rules, 2024: EPR Certificate Trading Platform and Price Bands Explained
item.description: On 8 March 2024, the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change notified the E-Waste (Management) Amendment Rules, 2024 — the third amendment to the 2022 framework. The amendment introduces a government-regulated marketplace for trading Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) certificates wit

item.title: E-Waste (Management) Second Amendment Rules, 2023: Refrigerants, RoHS Expansion & New Compliance Frontiers
item.description: On 24 July 2023, the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change notified the E-Waste (Management) Second Amendment Rules, 2023, introducing two transformative changes: mandatory safe and sustainable management of refrigerants from air-conditioning and refrigeration equipment (both at manufac
